metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback note (e.g., 'To revert: `git add .joe-janitor/ && git commit`; remove the `.git/info/exclude` entry') to the What/files section or a dedicated Rollback subsection.
findings.finding: The plan executes a destructive git operation (`git rm --cached` on committed files) and explicitly justifies it, but provides no rollback/back-out mechanism. T4 requires an explicit reversibility path, not just acknowledgement of the destructive step.
findings.location: What / files section — '`git rm --cached` the tracked `.joe-janitor/` files'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' subsection: 'To revert: `git add .joe-janitor/tools.md .joe-janitor/report-*.md && git commit -m "revert: re-track joe-janitor reports"`; remove the `.joe-janitor/` line from `.git/info/exclude`. The files are still present on disk so no data is lost.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note (or shell snippet) to confirm `.git/info/exclude` exists before appending (e.g., `echo '.joe-janitor/' >> .git/info/exclude`), since the file is not guaranteed present on all clones.
findings.finding: The plan names `.git/info/exclude` as an edit target but does not confirm it already exists on this machine. `.git/info/exclude` is auto-created by `git init` but may be absent or contain conflicting entries on clones that skipped the standard init hook. The plan provides no check-or-create step.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What/files section, replace the bare reference with: `echo '.joe-janitor/' >> .git/info/exclude  # file is auto-created by git init; this is safe to run on any clone`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Inspect `.dockerignore` for duplicate `dist/` / `build/` entries and either add it to the edit set or explicitly note it was checked and is clean.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate `.dockerignore` as a parallel edit target. `.dockerignore` is a sibling of `.gitignore` and may also carry duplicate `dist/` / `build/` entries that the plan's duplicate-removal rationale would logically extend to — leaving the `.dockerignore` unsynchronised is an unenumerated consumer of the same hygiene rule.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `-r` flag to the `git rm --cached` command so it reads `git rm --cached -r .joe-janitor/` (or enumerate the three files individually) to avoid a fatal Git error on directory removal.
findings.finding: The plan's `git rm --cached .joe-janitor/` command targets a directory but omits the required `-r` flag; Git will refuse with `fatal: not removing '.joe-janitor/' recursively without -r`, so the untrack step will fail without intervention.
findings.location: What / files — first bullet: `git rm --cached` the tracked `.joe-janitor/` files
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the command with `git rm --cached -r .joe-janitor/` or list the three files explicitly: `git rm --cached .joe-janitor/tools.md .joe-janitor/report-2026-06-24.md .joe-janitor/report-2026-07-02.md`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
